title: What was the message you got from EOE
display_title: What was the message you got from EOE
wikitext:
[]: What was the message you got from EOE? Post by TriLink » Mon Mar 19, 2007 5:38 pm So I decided to rewatch EOE the other day for the 1st time in a long time, right? It was a fairly uneventful viewing. Having seen it probably dozens of times before in an effort to understand it, I think I've just about maxed out MY understanding of Anno's mindfuck. However, after this latest viewing I was reminded of a fact that had somewhat faded in my mind in the last couple of months. It being that NGE depresses the hell out of me. After seeing EOE initially back in July, my reaction was like many of the others I've heard; mixed. Some people saw the uplifting message of finding happiness wherever you are, while others, like me, saw two kids sitting on a beach together totally screwed with little hope of surviving. Anyway, I was wondering how you guys felt after seeing EOE, and the TV series too. Did you see the positive message of being happy or two totally fucked kids whom will be miserable for the rest of their lives? And sorry if there is already a thread dedicated to this topic. I did a search to try and find some, and only found one that kinda fit the bill. It seemed a little too vague for what I was trying to ask so here we are.

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Mar 19, 2007 5:41 pm The message I got was "If you just fall into self-pity, don't be surprised if your world goes to hell around you."

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Mar 19, 2007 5:45 pm With both EoTV and EoE, I went away with the message of finding happiness wherever you are in that there IS hope after Depression and you can learn to Love yourself and others, as well as the fact that life is neither good (Asuka's caress) or bad (*coldest voice possible* "...kimochi warui."), but life just is .

[]: Post by Timstuff » Mon Mar 19, 2007 5:47 pm For me the message is "You can't love the people around you if you don't love yourself." Well, one of the messages, at least. There are a lot in there and I don't feel like typing for a half hour, so I pointed out what's one of the more obviouse ones to me. :P

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Mar 19, 2007 5:52 pm Also -- Even a Friendly Singularity will be a terrifying thing to experience.

[]: Post by BobBQ » Mon Mar 19, 2007 6:01 pm The moral of the story for me was this: Shit happens. Either you deal with it, or Darwin renders you obselete.

[]: Post by Reichu » Mon Mar 19, 2007 6:04 pm BobBQ wrote: Shit happens. Either you deal with it, or Darwin renders you obselete.

[]: Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Mon Mar 19, 2007 7:05 pm The message is , don't choose suicide over life. Admittedly it's a little hard to decipher this as the End of Days flashes by on screen, but that's it in a nutshell. See End of TV for a more/less obfuscated version of the message.

[]: Post by C4 » Mon Mar 19, 2007 9:21 pm The main message that I got out of it was, you can be a horrible person, you can do the most fucked up things, but in the end, no sin is unforgivable, and as long as you are willing to overcome the fear of having your heart penetrated by another's, you can find happiness even in the most desolate of situations.

[]: Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Mon Mar 19, 2007 9:22 pm Reichu wrote: BobBQ wrote: Shit happens. Either you deal with it, or Darwin renders you obselete.

[]: Post by BrikHaus » Mon Mar 19, 2007 10:58 pm Hasn't anybody considered the possibility that EoE was actually one huge, brilliant marketing ploy to make us all buy Tang? Mmmmmm, delicious orange Tang.

[]: Post by Defectron » Mon Mar 19, 2007 11:02 pm Hasn't anybody considered the possibility that EoE was actually one huge, brilliant marketing ploy to make us all buy Tang? Mmmmmm, delicious orange Tang.

[]: Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Mon Mar 19, 2007 11:15 pm BrickHaus81 wrote: Hasn't anybody considered the possibility that EoE was actually one huge, brilliant marketing ploy to make us all buy Tang? Mmmmmm, delicious orange Tang.

[]: Post by Reichu » Mon Mar 19, 2007 11:16 pm Defectron wrote: But it's made from people! People I tell you!!!! And possibly a warm water penguin thrown into the mix too! (penpen was in instrumentality)

[]: Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Mar 19, 2007 11:35 pm I've always wondered if there was ever any concrete message behind any of NGE. To me, it seemed like a series (and film) just filled with a lot of questions. I think that's why people have such radically different reactions to it. It's not because it's "open ended" or that it's "open to interpretation" exactly. I think the film is more of one big philosophical rumination than something that holds a single, catch-all "message". When a film/series is about a question rather than a concrete message, it's up to the viewer to answer that question. When they find the answer they want, that's what they latch onto, and that's what means something to them. It's not that that's what the film TOLD you, but it's what conclusion YOU came to by asking yourself the questions in the film. So for me, it's about the questions. "If you had to choose between life, and a non-existence where you are everywhere and nowhere, what would you choose?" "What makes life worth living at all?" "What is the point to all of life's hardships?" "If life gets too hard is it okay to 'run away'?" "At what point does your perception of life become your entire reality?" "What's the difference between your reality and actual reality?" These are NOT easy questions, and there's a ton more in the film/series. And for people to find meaning in the show, I think you have to ask yourself these questions and find an answer that means something to you. IMO, I think most of the most intelligent films and series are the ones that DON'T preach, but the ones that make you think. "A truly wise man always has more questions than answers", and NGE and EoE was a show filled with a ton of questions.

[]: Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Mon Mar 19, 2007 11:39 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: IMO, I think most of the most intelligent films and series are the ones that DON'T preach, but the ones that make you think. "A smarter man always has more questions than answers", and NGE and EoE was a show filled with a ton of questions.

[]: Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Mar 20, 2007 12:21 am THE Hal E. Burton 9000 wrote: And that's why, Eva is one of the greatest things ever...

[]: Post by Trigger's Elysium » Tue Mar 20, 2007 12:27 am Curious, what would these other works be?

[]: Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Mar 20, 2007 12:45 am Trigger's Elysium wrote: Curious, what would these other works be?

[]: Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Mar 20, 2007 1:07 am To add two more to the list, there's only been 2 video games that have ever really hit me. One was Silent Hill, mostly after reading the plot guide to it, but I still think it's brilliant. The other is Metal Gear Solid. I've wished for years they would turn that into a mini-series for HBO or something. I don't want to see either a fucked up Hollywood movie version nor an edited down, Basic Cable TV version as its plot is too intricate for either one.
